No Poverty.reason: The study of income tax, particularly "progressive tax rates" (累進稅率), is fundamental to funding social protection systems (Target 1.3) and creating pro-poor policy frameworks (Target 1.b).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course on "Theory and Practice of Income Taxation" supports strengthening domestic financial institutions, a key component of Target 8.10.
evidence: Theory and Practice of Income Taxation

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course explicitly covers "progressive tax rates" (累進稅率), a key fiscal policy mentioned in Target 10.4 for achieving greater equality and reducing income disparities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The topic "房地合一稅" (tax on consolidated income from housing and land) is a fiscal tool that tangentially relates to housing policy under Target 11.1.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course on "Theory and Practice of Income Taxation" is fundamental to building the effective, accountable, and transparent institutions described in Target 16.6.
evidence: Theory and Practice of Income Taxation

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course directly builds domestic capacity for tax collection (Target 17.1) and explicitly lists "Sustainable Development Goals, SDGs" as a foundational topic.
evidence: 聯合國永續發展目標(Sustainable Development Goals, SDGs)
